given: a warehouse operator, and a SKU that holds stock at more than one location
when: the SKU detail view for that SKU is opened
then: the view shows one entry for every location where that SKU holds stock, and only that SKU's records, each entry showing its location and quantity
architectural_notes: E2E: the React SPA detail page (client/) calls the app/routes boundary, which returns JSON only; app/services owns the read logic (fetch this SKU's stock_records and group one entry per location), app/repositories is the sole ORM layer and issues the WHERE sku=:sku read against the paired branch, app/models holds the domain entity. The 'only that SKU's records' scoping is enforced in the repository query, not filtered in the boundary or client. No new cross-cutting concern beyond the R4 real-branch read path; the multi-location result set is verified against seeded branch rows.
